title: Resolve conflicts by reconciling intent, not by picking a side
description: Read what each side changed and why before editing a conflict hunk, producing a result that preserves both intents rather than discarding one wholesale.
body: Use when a merge or rebase stops on conflicting hunks. Do not blindly accept ours or theirs to make the conflict disappear. Actions: inspect both versions with the merge base for context; edit the hunk so both changes survive where they are compatible; remove every conflict marker; re-run the build and tests before continuing. Validation signals include a passing test suite and a diff that reflects both branches' intent. Risk boundary: silently dropping one side's change can reintroduce a fixed bug, so verify behavior with a test rather than trusting the merge.
